entries.statement: Knowledge of data quality dimensions (accuracy, completeness, consistency, timeliness, uniqueness, validity, integrity) and the measurement and assessment approaches applicable to each dimension.

Knowledge of data standardization policies and procedures, including regulatory standards (ICH E6, 21 CFR Part 11, Annex 11), industry standards (CDISC CDASH, SDTM, ADaM), and submission dataset requirements for regulatory agencies.

Knowledge of clinical data management standards and requirements, including Good Clinical Practice (GCP) principles, Electronic Data Capture (eCDC) systems, clinical data coding conventions (MedDRA, WHO Drug Dictionary), and data integrity requirements for regulatory submissions.

Knowledge of data quality management program design, including profiling methodologies, rule authoring standards, quality threshold setting, issue classification and prioritization, escalation path design, and continuous improvement cycles.

Knowledge of root cause analysis methods for data quality failures, including source system analysis, data transformation logic review, process failure identification, and remediation strategy development.

Knowledge of data validation techniques and methods for embedding quality checks into pipeline execution, including schema validation, data contract enforcement, freshness checks, volume anomaly detection, referential integrity verification, and appropriate failure handling and alerting.

Knowledge of data quality testing frameworks and methodologies, including unit testing for data transformations, integration testing across data pipelines, regression testing for data integrity, and quality assurance approaches for production datasets.

Knowledge of master data management (MDM) principles and architecture, including golden record construction, data domain definition, MDM hub models (registry, consolidation, coexistence, centralized), and stewardship workflows.

Knowledge of entity resolution methods, including deterministic matching rules, probabilistic matching, machine learning-based matching, and the precision-recall trade-offs and identity management implications of each approach.

Knowledge of reference data management principles, including reference data taxonomy design, governance of controlled vocabularies, change management for reference data updates, and maintaining consistency across reference datasets.
Knowledge of metadata types (business, technical, operational, process) and the systems, methods, and tools used to collect, store, maintain, and govern each type throughout the data lifecycle.

Knowledge of data lineage concepts and documentation methods, including end-to-end lineage from source to consumption, transformation-level lineage, lineage visualization, and impact analysis use cases for data change management.

Knowledge of data catalog platform capabilities, including automated metadata harvesting, lineage visualization, search and discovery mechanisms, data classification, business glossary linkage, and impact analysis features.

Knowledge of database management system (DBMS) principles and practices, including data persistence, transaction management, index optimization, query performance tuning, backup and recovery procedures, and data retention policies.

Knowledge of data administration policies and procedures, including data lifecycle management, access control and security, data retention and archival, compliance documentation, and stewardship responsibilities.
Knowledge of data asset management principles and practices, including data discovery, asset inventory and cataloging, ownership assignment, lifecycle tracking, and value assessment of data assets.

Knowledge of data classification tools and techniques, including sensitivity classification (public, internal, confidential), risk-based data categorization, classification policy implementation, and use in access control and protection strategies.

Knowledge of data remediation tools and techniques, including data cleansing, standardization, deduplication, enrichment, imputation approaches, and quality improvement workflows for addressing identified data issues.

Knowledge of data privacy and minimization principles as operational data quality practices, including purpose limitation, consent management, legitimate interest assessment, data retention constraints, and personal data protection requirements.

Knowledge of data product design principles, including data product definition, consumer segmentation, interface design (APIs, subscriptions, files), Service Level Agreement (SLA) definition, and data product lifecycle management.

Knowledge of data mesh principles and federated data ownership models, including the role of data product thinking, domain ownership boundaries, platform infrastructure, and self-service analytics within decentralized data architectures.
